findings.title: Job hunter script silently fails when Apify token expires
findings.description: The job-hunter-apify.js script catches the Apify error but logs nothing useful. When the token expires, it appears to run successfully but finds 0 jobs.
findings.action: Add explicit token validation at startup and alert email on failure.

findings.title: LinkedIn token has no expiry check
findings.description: The .linkedin-token.json stores the access token but no expiry date. LinkedIn tokens expire after 60 days.
findings.action: Add expiry tracking and auto-refresh or alert before expiry.

findings.title: 47 temp files in Automation/temp/
findings.description: Old ZIP files from app deliveries are accumulating. Currently 47 files using 230MB.
findings.action: Add cleanup routine to deliver-app.js — delete temp files older than 7 days.

findings.title: Kaggle MCP server now available
findings.description: The Kaggle MCP server was released and could provide access to 50,000+ datasets and competition data directly from Claude Code.
findings.action: Install kaggle MCP server — requires kaggle.json API key.

findings.title: Auto-post AI news digest to LinkedIn weekly
findings.description: The daily AI news digest could be summarised weekly and posted to LinkedIn as a thought leadership post for NAVADA brand visibility.
findings.action: Create weekly LinkedIn auto-poster that summarises top 3 AI stories.

findings.action: Add expiry tracking and auto-refresh or alert before expiry.

findings.description: Old ZIP files from app deliveries are accumulating. Currently 47 files using 230MB.
findings.action: Add cleanup routine to deliver-app.js — delete temp files older than 7 days.

findings.description: The Kaggle MCP server was released and could provide access to 50,000+ datasets and competition data directly from Claude Code.
findings.action: Install kaggle MCP server — requires kaggle.json API key.

findings.title: Auto-post AI news digest to LinkedIn weekly
findings.description: The daily AI news digest could be summarised weekly and posted to LinkedIn as a thought leadership post for NAVADA brand visibility.
findings.action: Create weekly LinkedIn auto-poster that summarises top 3 AI stories.

findings.title: IMAP Connection Instability – inbox-responder & vc-monitor
findings.description: Both services show repeated ECONNRESET and connection timeout errors on 2026-02-28. inbox-responder.log reports 5+ IMAP failures in the last 3 hours; vc-monitor.log shows similar pattern. Root cause likely: Zoho Mail IMAP server drops idle connections or network instability. Impact: Email monitoring and VC response detection failing intermittently.
findings.action: Add exponential backoff retry (max 5 retries, 30s–5min delay) to IMAP connection handlers in Automation/inbox-responder.js and Automation/vc-response-monitor.js. Verify ZOHO_IMAP credentials in .env. Enable keep-alive on IMAP socket to prevent idle timeout.

findings.title: Voice Command System – Excessive Logging & Repeated Errors
findings.description: voice-command.log is 3.3MB with continuous [Errno -9999] 'Unanticipated host error' in listen loop since 2026-02-28 21:07. Service stuck in error loop, generating thousands of logs per hour. Root cause: Audio device driver issue or microphone disconnection. Impact: Log spam masks real issues; prevents diagnostics.
findings.action: Implement exponential backoff + circuit breaker in voice-command.js listen loop (max 3 consecutive errors → pause 60s). Add log rate limiting to 1 error per 10s. Restart voice service. Check audio device: Run `python -m sounddevice` to verify microphone connectivity.

findings.title: Claude Code Nested Session Error – Self-Improve Automation Broken
findings.description: self-improve.log (2026-02-28 20:40:45) shows 'Claude Code cannot be launched inside another Claude Code session.' This breaks the weekly Self-Improve-Weekly task (every Mon 10:00 AM). Ralph fallback partially works but produces incomplete findings (5 findings reported as stale). Impact: Automated codebase improvements not running.
findings.action: Refactor Automation/self-improve.js to skip nested Claude Code call. Use Ralph alone + direct file analysis (Glob + Grep) for findings. Call external `claude` binary via shell spawn with CLAUDECODE env var unset if nested session absolutely required.

findings.title: Node.js url.parse() Deprecation – CVE Risk in ai-news-digest
findings.description: ai-news-digest_20260226_070000.log shows DEP0169 deprecation warning: 'url.parse() behavior is not standardized and prone to errors that have security implications.' File likely: Automation/ai-news-mailer.js. Impact: Potential parsing vulnerabilities in URL handling.
findings.action: Replace all `url.parse()` calls with WHATWG URL API: `new URL(urlString)` in Automation/ai-news-mailer.js (and any other scripts using url.parse). Test with sample AI news URLs post-fix.

findings.title: Jupyter XSRF Token Validation Failure
findings.description: jupyter.log shows repeated HTTP 403 errors: '_xsrf' argument missing from POST to /api/kernels (2026-02-27 02:32:03). Indicates kernel API calls lack XSRF token or Jupyter security policy misconfigured. Impact: Kernel spawn requests blocked; Jupyter Lab unusable for programmatic access.
findings.action: Add XSRF token header to Jupyter kernel API calls. If using jupyter_client, upgrade to latest version. Alternatively, disable XSRF checks if running on localhost-only (--NotebookApp.disable_check_xsrf=True in jupyter config).

findings.title: Log File Bloat & Missing Rotation – voice-command.log at 3.3MB
findings.description: voice-command.log is 3.3MB (3M+ lines) filling disk. No PM2 log rotation configured. Other large logs: jupyter.log (567 lines but may grow). Impact: Disk space waste; slow diagnostics; difficulty identifying new errors in massive files.
findings.action: Configure PM2 ecosystem.config.js log rotation: Add `max_size: '10M'` and `max_file: 5` to voice-command and all long-running services. Restart PM2. Example: `{'name': 'voice-command', 'script': '...', 'max_size': '10M', 'max_file': 5}`.

findings.title: Docker Compose Version Attribute Deprecated
findings.description: infrastructure-startup.log (2026-02-28 00:57:23) warning: 'the attribute `version` is obsolete' in docker-compose.yml. Compose v2+ no longer uses version field. File: CLAUDE_NAVADA_AGENT/infrastructure/docker-compose.yml.
findings.action: Remove the `version:` line (likely `version: '3.8'` or similar) from infrastructure/docker-compose.yml. Test with `docker compose up -d` to confirm no errors.

findings.title: TensorBoard Dependency Missing – pkg_resources Error
findings.description: tensorboard.log shows `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'pkg_resources'`. TensorBoard import fails on startup. Impact: MLFlow dashboard & TensorBoard metrics unavailable.
findings.action: Run `pip install setuptools` to restore pkg_resources. If TensorBoard unused, disable it from PM2 ecosystem to reduce noise. Otherwise, verify TensorBoard version compatibility with Python 3.12 (current NAVADA Python version).

findings.title: Voice command service log explosion (3.3M lines)
findings.description: Automation/logs/voice-command.log has grown to 3.3M+ lines with repetitive '[STANDBY] Listen error: [Errno -9999] Unanticipated host error' entries. This is consuming massive disk space and degrading system performance. The error indicates the voice listener loop has no backoff/retry delay—it's failing and immediately retrying thousands of times per hour.
findings.action: 1) Stop voice-command service (pm2 stop voice-command). 2) Truncate the log: echo > Automation/logs/voice-command.log. 3) Fix Automation/voice-command.js: add exponential backoff to audio device error handling (min 5s delay between reconnection attempts). 4) Add log rotation: use pm2 logrotate or implement max file size check in script. 5) Restart and monitor.

findings.title: IMAP connection instability across email services
findings.description: Both inbox-responder.log and vc-monitor.log show repeated 'read ECONNRESET' and 'Timed out while connecting to server' errors starting ~2026-02-28T20:30Z. This pattern suggests a network/DNS/firewall issue affecting Zoho IMAP connectivity. Email monitoring is silently failing when connections drop.
findings.action: 1) Check network connectivity: ping zoho.com, test IMAP port 993 (telnet imap.zoho.com 993). 2) Verify firewall rules allow outbound 993/imap.zoho.com. 3) Add connection retry logic with exponential backoff in Automation/inbox-responder.js (currently has no retry). 4) Implement connection pooling to reuse IMAP connection. 5) Add Prometheus metric for IMAP connection health.

findings.title: Self-improve automation hitting max turns and timeout
findings.description: Automation/self-improve.js has multiple failures: (1) Error: Reached max turns (15) at 2026-02-28T20:45:55Z, (2) ETIMEDOUT spawn error at 2026-02-28T21:03:34Z, (3) Claude Code session error 'cannot be launched inside another Claude Code session'. The script spawns Claude Code as a subprocess, which breaks when running under Claude Code itself. Max turns indicates the scan completes but hits API rate limits.
findings.action: 1) Remove Claude Code spawning from self-improve.js—use direct API calls instead (Anthropic SDK). 2) Implement pagination for large diagnostics (split into batches if >50 log files). 3) Add timeout parameter (default 30s per request, configurable). 4) Cache diagnostics to avoid re-scanning on retry. 5) Add dry-run mode to test without spawning. Reference: Automation/self-improve.js line where it calls 'claude-code' subprocess.

findings.title: TensorBoard service broken—missing pkg_resources
findings.description: Automation/logs/tensorboard.log shows 'ModuleNotFoundError: No module named pkg_resources'. This is a setuptools dependency issue. TensorBoard is listed in infrastructure but non-functional, likely wasting resources or causing silent failures during model logging.
findings.action: 1) Stop TensorBoard: pm2 stop tensorboard (if running). 2) Run: pip install --upgrade setuptools. 3) Verify: py -m tensorboard --version. 4) If still broken, remove from PM2 until needed or replace with lightweight alternative (e.g., WandB for model tracking). 5) Test: py -m tensorboard --logdir ./mlruns --port 6006.

findings.title: Node.js url.parse() deprecation warning in production
findings.description: Automation/logs/ai-news-digest_20260226_070000.log shows deprecation warning: 'url.parse() behavior is not standardized and prone to errors...Use WHATWG URL API instead.' This will break in Node.js 24+. Currently suppressed but indicates outdated code pattern.
findings.action: 1) Find all url.parse() calls: grep -r "url.parse" Automation/ --include="*.js". 2) Replace with new URL() API: change 'url.parse(str)' to 'new URL(str)'. 3) Test each automation script after change. 4) Add '--trace-deprecation' to package.json scripts to catch other warnings. Reference: likely in ai-news-digest.js and any scrapers using URL parsing.

findings.title: Jupyter XSRF token validation failing
findings.description: Automation/logs/jupyter.log and jupyter-error.log show repeated 403 Forbidden errors: '_xsrf argument missing from POST'. Jupyter's XSRF protection is working, but clients attempting to create kernels aren't sending the token. This indicates either misconfigured Jupyter clients or API calls bypassing token validation.
findings.action: 1) Identify which client is making POST requests to /api/kernels without XSRF token. 2) Check if Jupyter is accessed via nginx reverse proxy (CLAUDE.md mentions nginx on :8080) — proxy may be stripping headers. 3) Verify nginx config in infrastructure/nginx/conf.d/default.conf includes: proxy_pass_request_headers on; proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for; 4) Alternatively, disable XSRF for localhost-only access: jupyter lab --NotebookApp.disable_check_xsrf=True (only if trusted network).

findings.title: Docker Compose schema version obsolete
findings.description: infrastructure-startup.log shows warning: 'the attribute `version` is obsolete, it will be ignored'. Docker Compose v2 no longer uses the version field. Current compose file is syntactically valid but outdated.
findings.action: 1) Edit infrastructure/docker-compose.yml. 2) Remove or update the 'version: "3.x"' line at the top (if present). 3) Ensure services are defined per v2+ spec. 4) Run: docker compose config to validate. 5) Test: docker compose up -d && docker compose ps.

findings.title: Infrastructure startup log encoding corruption
findings.description: infrastructure-startup.log tail shows null bytes and character encoding issues: '\u0000' repeated throughout (e.g., '2\u00000\u00020\u00026...'). This is likely a UTF-16 or binary encoding issue causing garbled output. Does not affect functionality but makes debugging harder.
findings.action: 1) Check how infrastructure startup logs are captured: likely shell redirection issue. 2) If using PowerShell, ensure UTF-8 encoding: [Console]::OutputEncoding = [System.Text.UTF8Encoding]::new(). 3) If using cmd.exe, ensure log file is created with UTF-8: chcp 65001. 4) Alternatively, pipe to 'Out-File -Encoding UTF8' in PowerShell. 5) Truncate corrupted log and restart containers.

findings.title: IMAP Connection Instability Crashing Email Services
findings.description: inbox-responder.log and vc-monitor.log show repeated ECONNRESET and timeout errors on IMAP connections (Feb 28 - Mar 2). This is causing email monitoring to fail intermittently and accumulating unhandled connection resets. Multiple services depend on stable IMAP connectivity (inbox-responder, vc-monitor, inbox-monitor).
findings.action: Implement IMAP connection pooling with exponential backoff retry logic in Automation/inbox-responder.js and vc-monitor.js. Add heartbeat/NOOP commands to keep connections alive. Consider upgrading node-imap to latest version and enabling connection timeout recovery.

findings.title: Voice Command Service Stuck in Error Loop (3.3M Log)
findings.description: voice-command.log has grown to 3.3 million lines with repeated '[STANDBY] Listen error: [Errno -9999] Unanticipated host error' every ~100ms. This indicates audio device failure but service is not exiting gracefully. Massive log file is consuming disk space and degrading system performance.
findings.action: Fix audio device initialization in voice-command service (likely C:\Users\leeak\Automation\voice-command.js or similar). Add error threshold to exit service after N consecutive failures instead of looping. Implement log rotation with max 10MB per file. Check Windows audio device availability before starting service.

findings.title: PDF Generation Fails on Non-ASCII Characters
findings.description: weekly-report_20260301_180000.log shows FPDFUnicodeEncodingException when gen-report.py tries to include titles with non-ASCII characters (Chinese, emoji, accents). Report generation fails completely, breaking weekly email digest.
findings.action: Update C:\Users\leeak\Alex\Automation\reports\gen-report.py to use Unicode font in FPDF. Change font from 'helveticaB' to 'DejaVu' or embed TrueType font. Test with sample non-ASCII content before next scheduled run.

findings.title: Deprecated Node.js url.parse() in ai-news-digest
findings.description: ai-news-digest_20260226_070000.log contains Node.js DEP0169 deprecation warning: 'url.parse() behavior is not standardized and prone to errors that have security implications'. Service is still functional but using vulnerable URL parsing method.
findings.action: Replace url.parse() calls with WHATWG URL API (new URL()) in Automation/ai-news-mailer.js. Run: grep -r 'url.parse' Automation/ to find all instances and update them.

findings.title: TensorBoard Won't Start - Missing pkg_resources Module
findings.description: tensorboard.log shows ModuleNotFoundError on startup. TensorBoard service is unavailable. This breaks ML experiment tracking if MLflow integration depends on it.
findings.action: Run: pip install setuptools (pkg_resources is part of setuptools). Verify: py -m tensorboard --version. If still failing, rebuild Python environment or use: pip install --upgrade tensorboard.

findings.title: Docker Compose Version Attribute Deprecated
findings.description: infrastructure-startup.log shows warning: 'version attribute is obsolete, it will be ignored, please remove it' from docker-compose. While not breaking functionality, this will fail in future Docker Compose versions.
findings.action: Remove 'version: 3' line from C:\Users\leeak\CLAUDE_NAVADA_AGENT\infrastructure\docker-compose.yml. Test: docker compose up -d from infrastructure/ directory.

findings.title: Jupyter Server XSRF Token Validation Errors
findings.description: jupyter-error.log shows multiple '403 POST /api/kernels' errors with missing _xsrf token (Feb 27 02:32). Indicates either XSRF protection misconfiguration or client not sending tokens properly. Could indicate security bypass vulnerability.
findings.action: Check Jupyter config in .jupyter/jupyter_server_config.py. Verify: c.ServerApp.disable_check_xsrf = False is NOT set. Ensure clients (like VS Code, Excalidraw) are sending XSRF tokens. Test with: curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8888/api/kernels (should fail with XSRF error, not 500).

findings.title: Add Centralized Log Rotation and Monitoring
findings.description: Multiple services accumulating large log files (voice-command.log 3.3M, infrastructure-startup.log corrupted). No centralized log management. Diagnostic report shows 'pm2 unavailable' and 'npm audit' command failures.
findings.action: Implement log rotation in PM2 ecosystem.config.js: add 'max_size: 10M' and 'max_file: 5' per app. Create C:\Users\leeak\Automation\logs\monitor.js to warn when logs exceed 50MB combined. Add to weekly-report task.

findings.title: Telegram Bot 409 Conflict - Chief of Staff Won't Start
findings.description: telegram-bot.log shows repeated startup failure: '409: Conflict: terminated by other getUpdates request'. Bot process may be hung or multiple instances running. This breaks the primary mobile interface for NAVADA.
findings.action: Run 'pm2 list' and 'pm2 delete telegram-bot && pm2 start Automation/telegram-bot.js --name telegram-bot'. If still fails, check for hung Node processes: 'tasklist | grep node' and kill orphaned telegram-bot instances.

findings.title: IMAP Connection Instability - Frequent ECONNRESET
findings.description: inbox-responder.log and vc-monitor.log show recurring 'read ECONNRESET' errors (9+ occurrences on 2026-03-04 alone). IMAP connections dropping unexpectedly, causing inbox monitoring and VC response monitoring to fail silently. Zoho IMAP may have connection pooling issues or timeout settings.
findings.action: Add connection retry logic with exponential backoff in Automation/inbox-responder.js and Automation/vc-response-monitor.js. Implement connection pooling with keep-alive. Consider adding idle timeout management (Zoho: IMAP IDLE support).

findings.title: Prospect Pipeline Script Path Error
findings.description: prospect-pipeline.js_20260303_093001.log shows 'LeadPipeline' is not recognized as command (exit code 9009). Scheduled task at 9:30 AM daily is failing silently because script uses relative path that doesn't resolve in Task Scheduler context.
findings.action: Update Automation/scheduled-tasks config or LeadPipeline/prospect-pipeline.js to use absolute path: 'C:\\Users\\leeak\\CLAUDE_NAVADA_AGENT\\LeadPipeline\\...' or fix cwd in PM2 config/Windows Task Scheduler.

findings.title: Jupyter XSRF Token Validation Failures
findings.description: jupyter.log shows HTTP 403 errors: '_xsrf argument missing from POST'. Jupyter security middleware rejecting legitimate kernel creation requests. This may be XSRF cookie mismatch or misconfigured security settings.
findings.action: Run jupyter lab from command line with: 'jupyter lab --ip=0.0.0.0 --allow-root --disable-xsrf --NotebookApp.allow_remote_access=true'. Or regenerate XSRF tokens by clearing browser cookies and restarting Jupyter server on port 8888.

findings.title: Weekly Report PDF Unicode Encoding Error
findings.description: weekly-report_20260301_180000.log shows FPDFUnicodeEncodingException. Special characters in report titles (em dashes, unicode chars per CLAUDE.md content rules) break PDF generation using helveticaB font which only supports latin-1.
findings.action: Update Automation/reports/gen-report.py to use Unicode-compatible font: 'pdf.add_font("DejaVu", "", "path/to/DejaVuSans.ttf")' before pdf.set_font(). Or sanitize input to remove unsupported characters.

findings.title: Deprecated url.parse() Usage in ai-news-digest.js
findings.description: ai-news-digest_20260226_070000.log: '[DEP0169] DeprecationWarning: url.parse() behavior is not standardized...prone to security implications'. This is a known CVE vector even if not disclosed.
findings.action: Replace 'url.parse()' with WHATWG URL API in Automation/ai-news-digest.js: change 'new URL(urlString)' instead of 'url.parse(urlString)'. Search codebase for other url.parse() usage.

findings.title: TensorBoard Missing pkg_resources Module
findings.description: tensorboard.log shows 'ModuleNotFoundError: No module named pkg_resources'. TensorBoard fails to start - likely setuptools not installed or corrupted. Affects ML model tracking infrastructure.
findings.action: Run: 'pip install --upgrade setuptools' and 'pip install --upgrade tensorboard'. Verify with 'py -m tensorboard --version'.

findings.title: Infrastructure Container Log Encoding Issues
findings.description: infrastructure-startup.log tail contains null bytes and control characters (\u0000\u0032\u0030...) indicating binary/encoding corruption in Docker output. Makes troubleshooting container startup difficult.
findings.action: Update infrastructure/docker-compose.yml to ensure UTF-8 encoding. Add 'environment: LANG=C.UTF-8' to container configs. Test with 'docker logs navada-proxy | file -' to verify encoding.

findings.title: Claude API session tokens exposed in PM2 environment
findings.description: CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ACCESS_TOKEN (live OAuth tokens) is stored in the PM2 process environment for all 6 monitored processes. These tokens are readable via `pm2 list --json`, written to PM2's on-disk process store, and visible to any user who can read PM2 state. Two distinct active tokens are visible in this dump.
findings.action: Strip CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ACCESS_TOKEN from PM2 env by adding it to the `filter_env` array in each process's pm2 config, then `pm2 save`. Rotate both exposed tokens immediately via Anthropic console.

findings.title: hp-cloudwatch-metrics crashing repeatedly (4 restarts, exit_code=1) [POSSIBLY STALE]
findings.description: hp-cloudwatch-metrics (pm_id: 20) has restarted 4 times with exit_code=1. Currently online but will keep cycling. Likely an unhandled exception or AWS credential/config issue in Automation/hp-cloudwatch-metrics.js.
findings.action: Check `pm2 logs hp-cloudwatch-metrics --err --lines 50` for the stack trace. Fix the root cause (likely a missing env var or AWS SDK error) in Automation/hp-cloudwatch-metrics.js.

findings.title: hp-health-monitor crashing repeatedly (3 restarts, exit_code=1)
findings.description: hp-health-monitor (pm_id: 23) has restarted 3 times with exit_code=1. Process runs from C:\Users\leeak\Alex\Automation\hp-health-monitor.js — this is the Alex repo path, not CLAUDE_NAVADA_AGENT, which may indicate it is running stale/wrong code. [VERIFIED: Service currently ONLINE]
findings.action: Run `pm2 logs hp-health-monitor --err --lines 50`. Verify whether Alex\Automation\hp-health-monitor.js is the correct file or if it should be repointed to CLAUDE_NAVADA_AGENT\Automation.

findings.title: 5 core services unreachable: elasticsearch, kibana, worldmonitor, worldmonitor-api, trading-api
findings.description: Live health probes return unreachable for all 5 services. These run on Oracle VM (elasticsearch/kibana) and EC2 (worldmonitor/worldmonitor-api) and HP (trading-api). A complete outage across 3 nodes simultaneously suggests either the health monitor itself is broken (see finding #3) or Tailscale connectivity is down.
findings.action: SSH into each node via Tailscale and verify: Oracle (`ssh navada-oracle`) for ELK status, EC2 for worldmonitor PM2 status, HP for trading-api. Check Tailscale peer connectivity first: `tailscale status`.

findings.title: cloudflare-metrics event loop blocked: p95 latency 3129ms
findings.description: cloudflare-metrics (pm_id: 21) has a p95 event loop latency of 3129ms — 12x higher than all other processes (~230-256ms). This indicates a synchronous blocking operation or a very slow async operation holding the loop, likely in the Cloudflare API polling logic in Automation/cloudflare-metrics.js. [VERIFIED: Service currently ONLINE]
findings.action: Review Automation/cloudflare-metrics.js for synchronous I/O (fs.readFileSync, JSON.parse on large payloads, or a long-running setInterval callback). Add `setImmediate` yields or break up the work into smaller async chunks.

findings.title: Heap usage critically high across 3 processes (89-92%)
findings.description: hp-health-monitor: 91.78%, telegram-bot: 89.73%, navada-flix: 89.57%. All three are near GC pressure thresholds. Combined with restart history on telegram-bot (2x) and hp-health-monitor (3x), these processes are likely OOMing or hitting GC stalls. Total heap across all 6 processes is ~130MB — modest, but fragmentation at 90%+ usage is problematic. [VERIFIED: Service currently ONLINE]
findings.action: For telegram-bot: check kb/telegram-memory.json size and whether conversation history is being pruned. For hp-health-monitor: review for memory leaks (unbounded arrays/maps). Set `--max-old-space-size=256` in node_args for processes showing >85% sustained heap usage.

findings.title: Disk space low: 16GB free (6.7%) on C:
findings.description: C: drive has 17,162,133,504 bytes free (~16GB) of 255GB total. At 6.7% free, Windows performance degrades and PM2 log rotation, SQLite WAL files, and Docker volumes risk filling the drive. The LeadPipeline .db-shm and .db-wal files in git status suggest active write pressure.
findings.action: Run `pm2 flush` to clear PM2 logs. Audit Docker volume usage (`docker system df`). Check Automation/logs/ for old log files. Consider setting PM2 log rotation: `pm2 install pm2-logrotate` with max_size 10M.

findings.title: cloudflare-metrics and cloudwatch-logs running from wrong working directory
findings.description: cloudflare-metrics has pm_cwd=C:\Users\leeak\Alex (not CLAUDE_NAVADA_AGENT\Automation). cloudwatch-logs has pm_cwd=C:\Users\leeak\Alex\Automation. Both exec_paths point to CLAUDE_NAVADA_AGENT. This means relative file paths (logs, .env, config files) resolve to wrong directories, silently misconfiguring both services. [VERIFIED: Service currently ONLINE]
findings.action: Stop both processes, update their PM2 config to set `cwd` to `C:\Users\leeak\CLAUDE_NAVADA_AGENT\Automation`, then `pm2 save`. Confirm with `pm2 show cloudflare-metrics` after restart.
